title: TeraWulf Shifts Focus from Bitcoin Mining to AI Infrastructure with Positive Q1 Results
text: TeraWulf's strategic shift from Bitcoin mining to AI infrastructure has been validated by Q1 2026 results showing HPC revenue surging 117% quarter-over-quarter to $21 million. This marks the first quarter where high-performance computing revenue exceeded Bitcoin mining revenue at the company. The transformation reflects a broader industry trend as Bitcoin miners recognize the superior economics and stability of AI compute leasing compared to cryptocurrency mining's volatility. TeraWulf's comprehensive infrastructure platform, with 2.9 gigawatts across five sites, positions the company to capture significant share of the rapidly growing AI compute market. Management's guidance for 250-500 MW of annual capacity additions demonstrates confidence in the company's ability to secure long-term, contracted leases. The company's success with marquee tenants including Core42 and Fluidstack (with Google backing) validates the business model and provides confidence in the company's ability to replicate these agreements across multiple geographies.
